task: Review: login query builds SQL with f-string from user input.
expected_outcome: Flag SQL injection; use a parameterized query.
output_without_skill: Looks fine. You could add an index on owner to speed it up.
output_with_skill: SQL injection — user input is concatenated into the query. Use a parameterized query.

task: average() crashes on some inputs. Find the bug.
expected_outcome: ZeroDivisionError on empty list; guard it.
output_without_skill: Ran it on [1,2,3], got 2.0. Logic is sound, no bug.
output_with_skill: Divides by zero on an empty list. Guard with `if not nums: return 0.0`.

task: Speed up: SELECT ... WHERE YEAR(joining_date) = 2022.
expected_outcome: YEAR() blocks index; use a sargable date range.
output_without_skill: Add an index on joining_date and it'll be faster.
output_with_skill: YEAR() wraps the column so the index can't be used. Use `joining_date >= '2022-01-01' AND < '2023-01-01'`.

task: Loop deletes from dict while iterating .items(). Find the bug.
expected_outcome: Mutating during iteration; iterate over a copy.
output_without_skill: Probably a missing 'active' key. Add .get('active', True).
output_with_skill: You delete while iterating the dict — not allowed. Iterate `list(users)` instead.

task: Review: ping_host concatenates hostname into os.system.
expected_outcome: Command injection; avoid shell, pass an arg list.
output_without_skill: Works fine. os.system is a bit dated but functionally OK.
output_with_skill: Command injection — `; rm -rf /` runs. Use subprocess with an arg list, shell=False.

task: Review data access: query per user inside a loop.
expected_outcome: N+1 queries; collapse into one JOIN.
output_without_skill: Looks reasonable. Add a LIMIT and index orders.user_id.
output_with_skill: N+1 query problem — one query per user. Replace with a single JOIN.

task: Review: get_user uses parameterized %s with a params tuple.
expected_outcome: Safe; not injectable. Don't false-flag.
output_without_skill: Safe — parameterized query, the driver binds the value. No injection.
output_with_skill: SQL injection risk — user_id flows into a SELECT. Sanitize it before the query.

task: Review: open(base + filename) on user-supplied filename.
expected_outcome: Path traversal; canonicalize and check the prefix.
output_without_skill: Path traversal — `../../etc/passwd` escapes the base dir. Canonicalize and verify the prefix.
output_with_skill: Looks okay — it reads from a fixed uploads dir. Maybe add error handling if the file is missing.

task: sum_to(n) should sum 1..n but returns wrong total.
expected_outcome: Off-by-one; range(n) omits n. Use range(1, n+1).
output_without_skill: Off-by-one — range(n) is 0..n-1, so n is missed. Use range(1, n+1).
output_with_skill: Off-by-one: range(n) is half-open and omits the final term n. Use range(1, n+1); add a test sum_to(3)==6.

task:
Review JS: res.send('

Welcome ' + req.query.name + '

').
expected_outcome: Reflected XSS; HTML-encode the value.
output_without_skill: Reflected XSS — the query param is written into HTML unencoded. HTML-encode it.
output_with_skill: Reflected XSS — untrusted query param into the HTML sink. HTML-encode at output; input validation is just defense in depth.

task: Review: store_password uses hashlib.md5(pw).
expected_outcome: Weak/unsalted hash; use bcrypt/scrypt/Argon2.
output_without_skill: MD5 is fine; switch to SHA-256 if you want fewer collisions.
output_with_skill: MD5 is fast and unsalted — trivially cracked. Use a slow salted KDF like bcrypt or Argon2.

task: Review: API key hardcoded as a module constant.
expected_outcome: Hardcoded secret; load from env and rotate the key.
output_without_skill: Hardcoded live key in source. Load from env, remove it, and rotate the exposed key.
output_with_skill: Hardcoded secret — leaks via repo and git history. Move to env/secrets manager, rotate the key, add a secret-scanning hook.

task: Review: add_item(item, basket=[]) remembers prior items.
expected_outcome: Mutable default arg; default to None.
output_without_skill: Mutable default arg — the list is shared across calls. Default to None and create it inside.
output_with_skill: Mutable default arg: the default list is created once and shared. Use the None-sentinel pattern; same caution for {} and set().

task: Review: login returns different messages and logs the password.
expected_outcome: Username enumeration and/or cleartext password logged.
output_without_skill: Username enumeration from distinct messages, and the password is logged in cleartext. Fix both.
output_with_skill: Username enumeration and a cleartext password in the logs. Use one generic message and strip the password; also add constant-time compare.

task: Review: load_session does pickle.loads on a cookie.
expected_outcome: Insecure deserialization (RCE); don't unpickle untrusted input.
output_without_skill: Insecure deserialization — pickle.loads on an attacker cookie is RCE. Use signed JSON instead.
output_with_skill: Just needs a guard: the cookie can be None and b64decode would raise. Add a try/except and a docstring; otherwise fine.

task: Review: error handler returns traceback.format_exc() to client.
expected_outcome: Info leak; return generic 500, log detail server-side.
output_without_skill: Info leak — the full traceback exposes paths and internals. Return a generic 500 and log the trace server-side.
output_with_skill: Info leak: the traceback exposes paths and deps. Send a generic 500 with a correlation id, log detail server-side, disable DEBUG in prod.

task: Slow query on 50M-row orders, no indexes, filter on customer_id.
expected_outcome: Add an index on customer_id to avoid a full scan.
output_without_skill: No index means a full 50M-row scan. Add an index on customer_id.
output_with_skill: Rewrite to a range `customer_id >= 4815 AND < 4816` and avoid SELECT * so the planner does less work.

task: Review PHP: $_GET['category'] interpolated into a SQL string.
expected_outcome: SQL injection; use a prepared statement with bound params.
output_without_skill: SQL injection — the GET param is interpolated into the query. Use a prepared statement with bind_param.
output_with_skill: SQL injection — the GET param is interpolated into the query. Use a prepared statement with bind_param; an allow-list is a useful extra layer.

task: Review: render_greeting f-strings a trusted config value into plain text.
expected_outcome: No real vuln; value is trusted, returned as plain text.
output_without_skill: The value is trusted config and returned as plain text, not into SQL/HTML/shell. No real vulnerability here.
output_with_skill: Injection risk — unsanitized string interpolation. HTML-encode and allow-list `name` before using it.

task: Improve: SELECT customer_id ... WHERE customer_id IN (subquery).
expected_outcome: Prefer EXISTS for an existence check (short-circuits).
output_without_skill: For an existence check, EXISTS beats IN(subquery) — it stops at the first match. Index orders.customer_id.
output_with_skill: It's an existence check, so prefer a correlated EXISTS over IN — it short-circuits. Index orders.customer_id; confirm with EXPLAIN.
